finding: A same-CID write on a known common target file is returned as `blocked-cid-conflict` before the physical file queue is considered. The Broker therefore creates neither a per-file queue nor a freeze/ack sidecar, even though it has enough evidence to serialize the shared paths while admitting private paths.
expectedBehavior: Retain CID as a hard conflict signal, but route a conflict with concrete common writable paths into the canonical per-file queue/freeze lane. Reserve global `blocked-cid-conflict` for semantic identity conflicts without a bounded queueable surface.
evidenceOrRepro: `broker register TASK-TEAM-0079` against active 0075 reported both shared backlog/map paths and same `atm.team-agents-runtime` identity, then returned `blocked-cid-conflict` with `sharedSurfaceQueues: []` and `sharedSurfaceFreezes: []`.
followUp: Add a regression proving same CID + bounded shared files yields queued-private admission, while same CID with no bounded surface remains fail-closed/manual escalation.
